title: Vitis shuttleworthii
taxonomy: Vitis shuttleworthii is classified as a species of grapevine native to China. It belongs to the family Vitaceae, commonly known as the grape family. The genus Vitis includes over 60 species of grapevines, with Vitis shuttleworthii being one of the lesser-known varieties. Vitis shuttleworthii is often associated with the V. amurensis complex, a group of species that share similar characteristics. Further classification within the genus Vitis is often based on morphological and genetic analysis.

intro: Vitis shuttleworthii, also known as the Shuttleworth grapevine, is a species of grapevine native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may help to alleviate various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Vitis shuttleworthii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
